summary:
The prevalence of pit and fissure caries can be accurately assessed using the International Caries Detection and Assessment System (ICDAS), known for its ability to identify both non-cavitated and cavitated lesions; no other method matches this tool’s level of detail and diagnostic accuracy. This makes it essential for comprehensive evaluation and management strategies targeting such dental issues. To summarize concisely while focusing only on critical aspects related directly to answering your query about what measures the prevalence of pit and fissures specifically involves—ICDAS remains unparalleled given its thoroughness and precision across all stages of lesion development. Hence,

\(\boxed{\text{ICDAS}}\) serves best here because it provides an accurate assessment covering various stages from pre-disease through fully developed conditions within pits and fissures.
